column2: Description (This field is optional, you can leave it blank if you want to.)
column3: Document Content (Mandatory, set one of the following values: 0 for Use Content, 1 for Use Attachments, 2 for Use URL)
column4: Content Editor Text (This field is mandatory only if Document Content is set to "Use Content".)
column5: URL (This field is mandatory only if Document Content is set to "URL".)
column6: GRC Contact (Mandatory. Accepts multiple user logins or group names separated by "|". For User login use prefix "User-" and for Group name use "Group-". For example "User-admin|Group-Third Party Feedback|Group-Admin". You can get the login of an user account from System / Settings / User Management or name of a group from System / Settings / Groups.)
column7: Policy Reviewer Contact (Mandatory. Accepts multiple user logins or group names separated by "|". For User login use prefix "User-" and for Group name use "Group-". For example "User-admin|Group-Third Party Feedback|Group-Admin". You can get the login of an user account from System / Settings / User Management or name of a group from System / Settings / Groups.)
column8: Tags (Optional and accepts tags separated by "|". For example "Critical|SOX|PCI".)
column9: Published Date (Mandatory, it must follow the format YYYY-MM-DD, not the "-" character is used as delimiter.)
column10: Next Review Date (Mandatory, it must follow the format YYYY-MM-DD, not the "-" character is used as delimiter.)
column11: Policy Labels (Optional, set the value of the name of the label you want to use. Label names can be obtained from Asset Management / Asset Identification / Settings / Labels.)
column12: Related Projects (Optional and accepts multiple names separated by "|". You need to enter the name of a project, you can find them at Security Operations / Project Management.)
column13: Status (Mandatory, set value: 0 for Draft, 1 for Published)
column14: Document Type (You need to provide the name of the policy type, you can obtain the name of type from Control Catalogue / Policies / Settings / Document Type.)
column16: Policy Portal Permissions for this Document (Mandatory, set one of the following values: public for Public (Everyone can see the document), private for Private (Document is not shown on the portal), custom-roles for Only specific groups)
column17: Custom Roles (Mandatory in case you set permission to custom-roles, you can set more of following values: Owners for GRC Contact, Collaborators for Policy Reviewer Contact)
column18: Related Documents (Optional and accepts multiple names separated by "|". You need to enter the name of a policy, you can find them at Control Catalogue / Policies.)

column4:
Introduction


This policy defines the principles and procedures that guide our robust Logging and Monitoring program, focusing on enhancing our security framework through vigilant supervision.

Objectives


• Ensure comprehensive collection and secure retention of all pertinent system logs.
• Promptly identify, alert, and delve into any anomalous or unusual events.
• Uphold the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of the organization's information systems.
• Aggregate and correlate event data from multiple sources and sensors to enhance detection and analysis of anomalies.
• Regularly test and confirm the effectiveness of methods used for data discovery, particularly for detecting cleartext Primary Account Number (PAN) across all system components and file formats.

Scope


Covering all aspects, this policy includes every system and application in our operational sphere. This extends to in-depth logging of activities in our cloud infrastructure, capturing system logs from EC2 Ubuntu instances, and channeling specific application logs to CloudWatch. It also encompasses aggregating these logs with other data sources, such as network traffic monitors and external threat feeds, to provide a comprehensive view.

Standard


Configuration of Logging and Monitoring Systems


• Each target system and application should be adequately configured to record essential information.
• Systems should be equipped to aggregate and correlate data from various sources, enhancing the detection of potential threats.
• Log storage must ensure data remains confidential, trustworthy, and accessible, shielding against unauthorized changes.
• Presently, logs are preserved indefinitely for detailed inspections and forensic assessments. These logs are securely discarded after assessment, following a predetermined period.

Monitoring of Logs


• A custom script is initiated every five minutes, identifying particular event patterns in CloudWatch.
• An advanced correlation engine should analyze logs from different sources, identifying complex patterns indicative of sophisticated threats.
• Once an event matches set rules, an immediate notification should be directed to the designated channel.
• Pertinent staff from IT, cybersecurity, software development, management, or other relevant departments must acknowledge this alert, initiating a comprehensive probe.
• The results of such investigations are documented and communicated via Slack.

Incident Response and Investigation


• All incidents require an in-depth analysis, adhering strictly to guidelines in the Incident Management Policy.
• The outcomes of these investigations should be thoroughly recorded, influencing future refinements of the logging and monitoring systems.

Log Review and Analysis


• Logs should be periodically examined and analyzed to identify potential security threats and confirm the seamless operation of logging and monitoring setups.
• Any inconsistencies or potential enhancements identified during this log evaluation must be systematically recorded and subsequently addressed.

Data Discovery Effectiveness Testing and Confirmation


• The effectiveness of data discovery methods must be tested and confirmed at least once every 12 months.
• Documentation of these tests, including their scope, methods, and results, must be maintained for review and compliance purposes.


Monitoring and Review


Regular updates to this policy are paramount, aiming for at least an annual revision or whenever there are significant changes to the systems or processes.
The policy's effectiveness rests on frequent audits and assessments, ensuring it remains relevant and adapts with the shifting security landscape.
